Quality Education.reason: Course promotes understanding of WTO mechanisms, relevant to Target 4.7 on sustainable development education.
evidence: familiarize students with the unique WTO mechanism

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Course addresses trade disputes, linking to Target 8.3 on promoting productive activities.
evidence: settling trade disputes based on substantive trade rules

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Course focuses on WTO dispute resolution, relevant to Target 16.3 on promoting rule of law.
evidence: rule-based approach for the solution of trade disputes

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Course emphasizes multilateral cooperation in trade, linking to Target 17.10 on a rules-based trading system.
evidence: WTO Members use such multilateral dispute settlement system
solution of trade disputes among WTO Members

No Poverty.reason: International trade rules can indirectly support pro-poor development strategies (Target 1.b), but the course focuses on legal procedures, not poverty eradication policies.
evidence: Settlement of International Trade and Commercial Disputes
procedural issues in the WTO dispute settlement

Zero Hunger.reason: Directly addresses Target 2.b ('prevent trade restrictions... in world agricultural markets') through case studies on agricultural products like dairy, sugar, and cotton.

Quality Education.reason: As a graduate-level university course, it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops specialized professional knowledge, but does not focus on improving educational systems.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Analyzes the WTO framework (GATT, GATS), the rules-based system for global trade which is a key driver of economic growth and productivity (Target 8.1, 8.2).
evidence: General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT)
General Agreement on Trade in Services (GATS)

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The study of trade rules under GATT relates to industrial policy and market integration (Target 9.2, 9.3), but the course focuses on disputes, not industrial promotion.
evidence: General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Directly relates to Target 10.a, which explicitly mentions implementing special treatment for developing countries in accordance with 'World Trade Organization agreements'.
evidence: Introduction to WTO Dispute Settlement System

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The 'Diamonds Case' study on the 'Kimberley Process Certification Scheme' directly addresses the sustainable management and trade of natural resources (Target 12.2).

Life Below Water.reason: Target 14.6 explicitly mentions the 'World Trade Organization fisheries subsidies negotiation'. The course covers the WTO dispute system, which is the venue for such issues.

Life on Land.reason: The 'Diamonds Case' and 'Kimberley Process' relate to combating illicit trade in natural resources, thematically linked to preventing trafficking and protecting ecosystems (Target 15.7, 15.c).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is fundamentally about promoting the rule of law internationally (Target 16.3) through an effective, accountable global institution (WTO) (Target 16.6, 16.8).
evidence: rule-based approach for the solution of trade disputes
Promote the rule of law at the national and international levels

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course is a deep analysis of the WTO, the key institution for the 'rules-based... multilateral trading system' mentioned in Target 17.10, a cornerstone of global partnership.
evidence: The dispute settlement system in the WTO has played a crucial role
